game_concepts.Faith Resource System:
**Faith** is the core resource of Testament — analogous to Mana in Magic: The Gathering.

**Faith Symbols by Faction:**
- ✦ Celestial Host — White Faith (purity, protection, healing)
- ☽ Fallen Dominion — Black Faith (sacrifice, power, darkness)
- 📜 Prophetic Order — Blue Faith (knowledge, control, time)
- ⚡ Ancient Pantheon — Red Faith (raw power, chaos, destruction)
- ☸ Dharmic Path — Green Faith (nature, balance, growth)
- ⚖ Covenant Keepers — Multi-Faith (hybrid, versatile)

**Generating Faith:**
- Shrines produce 1 Faith per turn when tapped
- Players start with 3 Shrines in play; may play 1 Shrine per turn from hand
- Maximum 7 Shrines in play at once

**Faith Costs:**
- Generic Faith (any color): {1}, {2}, {3}...
- Colored Faith: {✦}, {☽}, {📜}, {⚡}, {☸}, {⚖}
- Example: {3}{✦}{✦} = 3 generic + 2 Celestial Faith
- Hybrid costs: {✦/☽} can be paid with either symbol
game_concepts.Card Types:
**SERVANT** (Creature equivalent)
Has ATK and DEF. Costs Faith to summon. Tribute rules: 5-6★ needs 1 Tribute; 7-8★ needs 2.

**PRAYER** (Sorcery equivalent)
Cast only during your Main Phase. Resolves immediately; goes to Graveyard after use.

**MIRACLE** (Instant equivalent)
Cast at ANY time, including opponent's turn and in response to other cards.

**COVENANT** (Enchantment equivalent)
Permanent card with an ongoing effect. Stays in play until destroyed.

**RELIC** (Artifact/Equipment)
Sacred objects, holy weapons. Some attach to Servants (+ATK/DEF). Some have activated abilities.

**SHRINE** (Land equivalent)
Generates 1 Faith of its color each turn when tapped. Free to play (1 per turn from hand).

**DIVINE TRAP** (Yugioh-style Trap)
Played face-down during your turn. Triggered by specific conditions on opponent's turn.
Set up to 3 Divine Traps face-down in your Trap Zone at a time.
game_concepts.Turn Structure:
**PHASE 1 — DAWN (Draw)**
Draw 1 card. If your deck is empty, you cannot draw (Judgment loss condition).

**PHASE 2 — FAITH (Resource)**
Untap all your tapped Shrines and Servants. Gain Faith from all untapped Shrines.
Faith pool resets each turn — unspent Faith does not carry over.

**PHASE 3 — MAIN (Action)**
Play Servants, Prayers, Covenants, Relics. Set Divine Traps face-down (free).
Place 1 Shrine from hand (free, once per turn). Activate abilities of permanents.

**PHASE 4 — BATTLE (Combat)**
Declare attackers (tap each). Opponent declares blockers.
Unblocked attackers deal ATK damage directly to opponent's Life Points.
Blocked combat: compare ATK vs DEF simultaneously.

**PHASE 5 — END (Cleanup)**
Discard down to 7 cards if over hand limit. Resolve end-of-turn effects.
Pass priority to opponent — their turn begins.
game_concepts.Win Conditions:
**1. DOMINION (Life Points)**
Reduce opponent's Life Points from 8000 to 0 through combat or card effects.

**2. JUDGMENT (Deck-Out)**
If a player must draw but their deck is empty, they lose immediately.
Prophetic Order specializes in this strategy.

**3. THE DIVINE RECKONING (Legendary Condition)**
Control all 5 of a faction's named Legendary Servants simultaneously.
Opponent has 1 full turn to disrupt after the 4th Legendary is played.
Only 1 copy of each Legendary allowed per deck.
game_concepts.Tribute & Rarity:
**TRIBUTE SYSTEM**
- ★★★★ or below: Normal Summon (no Tribute)
- ★★★★★–★★★★★★: Tribute Summon — sacrifice 1 Servant
- ★★★★★★★–★★★★★★★★: Tribute Summon — sacrifice 2 Servants
Only 1 Tribute Summon per turn.

**RARITY TIERS**
- ◆ Common (50%) — 1-3 Faith cost, ATK up to 1600, 1 ability
- ◇ Uncommon (30%) — 2-5 Faith cost, ATK up to 2400, 2 abilities
- ◈ Rare (15%) — 4-7 Faith cost, ATK up to 3000, 2 abilities
- ◉ Legendary (5%) — 6-9 Faith cost, ATK up to 4000, 3 abilities, max 1 copy per deck

**DECK BUILDING**
40–60 cards total. Max 3 copies of non-Legendary cards. Minimum 15 Shrines recommended.

abilities.name: Consecrated
abilities.rules: Cannot be targeted by opponent's spells or abilities.

abilities.name: Blessed
abilities.rules: Whenever this Servant deals combat damage, you gain that much Life Points.

abilities.name: Herald
abilities.rules: When this enters: reveal top card of deck. If same faction Servant, put it in hand.

abilities.name: Smite
abilities.rules: When this deals combat damage to a Servant, that Servant is destroyed at end of combat.

abilities.name: Ascend
abilities.rules: Special Summon from Graveyard by paying {2} and revealing 3 faction cards in hand.

abilities.name: Martyr
abilities.rules: When destroyed and sent to Graveyard: draw 1 card and gain 2 Life Points.

abilities.name: Fallen
abilities.rules: Counts as both Celestial Host and Fallen Dominion for all game effects.

abilities.name: Sanctify
abilities.rules: Once per turn: remove 1 Curse counter from a Servant you control. It gains +200 DEF.

abilities.name: Divinity
abilities.rules: Cannot be destroyed by effects with a Faith cost of 3 or less.

abilities.name: Pilgrimage
abilities.rules: When this enters: search your deck for a Shrine and put it into play tapped.

abilities.name: Exorcise
abilities.rules: Can block Fallen Dominion Servants for free. When it blocks a Demon, that Servant is banished.

abilities.name: Repent
abilities.rules: At start of your turn, pay 2 Life Points to remove all negative counters from this.

abilities.name: Wrath
abilities.rules: Gets +600 ATK while your Life Points are 3000 or less.

abilities.name: Covenant
abilities.rules: This card's effects cannot be negated.

abilities.name: Invoke
abilities.rules: Special Summon from hand by banishing 2 Servants of its faction from Graveyard.

abilities.name: Radiant
abilities.rules: All Servants of your faction gain +200 ATK while this Servant is on the field.

abilities.name: Transcend
abilities.rules: Cannot be targeted the turn it was summoned. +500 ATK if summoned from Graveyard.

abilities.name: Sacrifice
abilities.rules: When Tributed for a Tribute Summon, the summoned Servant enters with 2 extra +100 ATK counters.

abilities.name: Undying Faith
abilities.rules: When this would be destroyed, return it to hand instead (once per turn).

abilities.name: Prophesy
abilities.rules: Once per turn: look at the top 2 cards of your deck. Put them back in any order.

abilities.name: Meditate
abilities.rules: Cannot attack. DEF equals ATK + 500 while in Defense Position.

abilities.name: Dharmic
abilities.rules: If this deals damage in excess of what destroys a Servant, distribute excess to another target.

abilities.name: Eternal
abilities.rules: Cannot be banished. When it would be banished, goes to Graveyard instead.

abilities.name: Corrupted
abilities.rules: Enters with 1 Corruption counter. Add 1 each turn. At 5 counters, this is destroyed.

abilities.name: Divine Shield
abilities.rules: First time this would be destroyed each turn, prevent it and remove this keyword.

abilities.name: Banish
abilities.rules: When this destroys a Servant in combat, banish that Servant instead of Graveyard.

abilities.name: Zealot
abilities.rules: Must attack each turn if able. Gains +500 ATK during the Battle Phase.

abilities.name: Vigilance
abilities.rules: Does not tap when it attacks.

abilities.name: Flash
abilities.rules: May be summoned during opponent's turn or during your Battle Phase.

abilities.name: Soul Bind
abilities.rules: If this is destroyed, opponent must also destroy one of their Servants.

abilities.name: Heavenly Descent
abilities.rules: When ~ enters: Create two 1000/1000 Seraph tokens with Flying.

abilities.name: Death Knell
abilities.rules: When ~ is destroyed: Deal 1000 damage to all opponents and each discards 1 card.

abilities.name: Oracle's Vision
abilities.rules: Whenever you cast a Prayer: Draw 1 card. If it is a Prayer, cast it for free.

abilities.name: Thunder Strike
abilities.rules: Whenever ~ attacks: Deal 500 damage to target Servant or player.

abilities.name: Karmic Return
abilities.rules: Whenever opponent's effect would deal damage to you: Gain that much life instead.

abilities.name: Interfaith Bond
abilities.rules: Whenever you play a Servant of a different faction: +200/+200 Unity counter on ~.

abilities.name: Resurrection Call
abilities.rules: When ~ is destroyed: Return target Servant ATK 1500 or less from Graveyard to battlefield.

abilities.name: Demonic Possession
abilities.rules: When ~ enters: Take control of target opponent's Servant until ~ leaves play.

abilities.name: Prophetic Warning
abilities.rules: At start of opponent's Combat Phase: Look at their hand. Choose 1 Servant — it cannot attack.

abilities.name: Cataclysm
abilities.rules: When ~ is destroyed: Destroy all Servants with ATK less than ~'s ATK.

abilities.name: Lotus Bloom
abilities.rules: At upkeep: Gain 1 life and add {☸}. If you have 10+ life, draw 1 card instead.

abilities.name: Sacred Covenant
abilities.rules: When ~ enters: Search deck for a Covenant card and put it into hand.

abilities.name: Holy Judgment
abilities.rules: {2}{✦}: Destroy target Servant with ATK 2000 or less. Main Phase only.

abilities.name: Soul Harvest
abilities.rules: {☽}: Sacrifice a Servant. Gain life equal to that Servant's ATK / 100.

abilities.name: Arcane Study
abilities.rules: {1}{📜}: Draw 2 cards, then discard 1. Main Phase only.

abilities.name: Divine Fury
abilities.rules: {⚡}{⚡}: This Servant gains +1000 ATK until end of turn. Must attack if able.

abilities.name: Inner Peace
abilities.rules: {☸}: Remove all negative effects and counters. Cannot be targeted until your next turn.

abilities.name: Interfaith Rally
abilities.rules: {⚖}{⚖}: All Servants gain +500 ATK, or +1000 if you control 3+ different factions.

abilities.name: Counter Spell
abilities.rules: {1}{📜}: Counter target Prayer or Miracle. Activate when a Prayer or Miracle is cast.

abilities.name: Thunder God's Wrath
abilities.rules: {⚡}{⚡}{⚡}: Deal 2000 damage to target Servant. 3000 if it has Consecrated.

abilities.name: Zen Focus
abilities.rules: {1}{☸}: Damage you would take is reduced by 500 until end of turn (minimum 0).

abilities.name: Diplomatic Immunity
abilities.rules: {⚖}: Target Servant cannot be targeted until your next turn. Activate on opponent's turn.

abilities.name: Celestial Aura
abilities.rules: All Celestial Host Servants you control get +300 ATK and +300 DEF.

abilities.name: Lord of Darkness
abilities.rules: All Fallen Dominion Servants get +500 ATK. Each opponent's Servant loses 200 ATK.

abilities.name: Forbidden Knowledge
abilities.rules: Prayers and Miracles you cast cost {1} less (minimum {1}).

abilities.name: Olympian Presence
abilities.rules: Opponent's Servants lose 200 ATK for each God-type Servant you control.

abilities.name: Enlightened Path
abilities.rules: At start of your turn, if you have more life than opponent, draw an extra card.

abilities.name: Bridge of Faith
abilities.rules: You may use Faith of any color to pay the colored costs of multi-faction cards.

abilities.name: Holy Ground
abilities.rules: Fallen Dominion Servants lose 500 ATK while on this field. Cannot be negated.

abilities.name: Seeker of Truth
abilities.rules: Your hand size maximum is 10 instead of 7.

abilities.name: Karmic Balance
abilities.rules: If your life is less than opponent's, Servants get +200 ATK. If greater, +200 DEF.

abilities.name: Pantheon Lord
abilities.rules: Whenever a Servant you control deals damage, that damage is increased by 200.

Celestial Host ✦.lore: The Celestial Host are the armies of Heaven — angels, archangels, seraphim, and mortal saints elevated to divine service. Organized under a hierarchy of perfect order, they exist to protect the sacred, heal the wounded, and purge corruption. Their Faith is blazing divine light that reveals and burns away all that is false. They do not hate their enemies; they execute judgment with love, believing that even destruction can be an act of mercy.
figures: Commander of Heaven's armies; Sword of Truth; anti-Demon specialist
The Herald; divine messenger; strongest Prayer/Miracle synergy
The Healer; life restoration master; key to healing-win strategies
The Flame of God; guardian; exile specialist
Six-winged; creates Seraph tokens; swarm enabler
Heaven's gatekeeper; prevents combat damage to allies
Celestial Host ✦.playstyle: Protective and resource-efficient. Excels at maintaining board presence and grinding opponents down. Protects key Servants with Consecrated and Divine Shield, heals back damage, and uses exile effects to permanently remove threats.
strengths: Outstanding defensive capabilities
Strong anti-Fallen Dominion exile effects
Excellent card advantage through Herald chains
Life gain prevents Dominion loss condition
Consecrated blocks many removal spells
weaknesses: Relatively low ATK — relies on evasion over raw power
Weak against Ancient Pantheon's non-targeted board wipes
Expensive top-end requires careful Shrine building
Celestial Host ✦.counter: Attack the mana base — destroy Shrines early. Use mass effects that hit all Servants simultaneously (they bypass Consecrated). Fill their Graveyard then banish it to cut off Ascend recursion.

Fallen Dominion ☽.lore: Once among Heaven's most radiant servants, the Fallen chose pride over obedience and were cast into the abyss — not broken, but transformed. The Fallen Dominion is a realm of terrible beauty and absolute power, where strength is earned through sacrifice. They believe true power requires the willingness to consume all — even oneself — in its pursuit.
figures: Fallen general; master of corruption; steals opponent's Servants
Embodies Greed; generates massive Faith from sacrifices
Destruction specialist; high ATK glass cannon
Former angel; retains angelic beauty with demonic power; dual-faction
Fallen Dominion ☽.playstyle: High-risk, high-reward aggression and sacrifice. Sacrifices weaker Servants to fuel more powerful ones, drains opponent's resources through hand disruption and life damage, and establishes overwhelming board states through sheer destructive force.
strengths: Highest raw ATK values
Graveyard recursion makes threats hard to permanently remove
Hand disruption cripples control strategies
Sacrifice mechanics generate resource advantages
weaknesses: Low DEF — vulnerable to being outpaced
Heavily countered by Celestial Host exile effects
Fallen Dominion ☽.counter: Banish everything — don't let cards go to the Graveyard. Heal aggressively to weather early bursts. Force them to sacrifice too early; they will run out of fuel.

Prophetic Order 📜.lore: The Prophetic Order are keepers of divine revelation — prophets, apostles, scribes, sages, and oracles who have been granted visions of what was, what is, and what will be. They do not fight with brute force; they fight with information, timing, and the art of ensuring that every move their opponent makes leads to ruin.
figures: Sees 5 turns ahead; Prophesy passive; perfect sequence setup
Resilient blocker with hand-refill abilities; defensive cornerstone
Every 5 cards drawn triggers a free Miracle
Has built-in Counter Spell on his body
Prophetic Order 📜.playstyle: Plays the long game. Draws cards constantly, counters key plays, and slowly builds insurmountable advantage. Aims for Judgment (deck-out) while protecting itself with Flash Servants and instant-speed Miracles.
strengths: Unmatched card advantage
Counterspells negate the most powerful effects
Judgment win condition is completely different to play around
Flash Servants are unpredictable
weaknesses: Low ATK — vulnerable to pure aggression
Judgment strategy fails if opponent has library protection
Weak against Fallen Dominion's hand disruption
Prophetic Order 📜.counter: Relentless aggression. Force them to spend counterspells on creatures. Cards that say 'this cannot be countered' are hard counters. Speed — win before turn 6.

Ancient Pantheon ⚡.lore: Before the current age, other gods held sway — the Greek Olympians, Norse Aesir, Egyptian Ennead, Aztec Teteo. The Ancient Pantheon is not united — it is a gathering of ancient powers who share only their immense, barely-contained strength. They do not plan. They do not need to. They simply are — and their existence alone reshapes the world.
figures: King of Olympians; direct damage to any target every turn
Wisdom and war; Prophesy on a body with war-god ATK
Egyptian sun god; heals equal to damage dealt each turn
Feathered Serpent; mass destruction on summon
Board wipe as an activated ability
Ancient Pantheon ⚡.playstyle: Pure power faction. Servants have the highest combined ATK. Overwhelm with sheer force, punish blockers with Cataclysm triggers, use non-targeted effects to bypass Consecrated.
strengths: Highest ATK stats
Non-targeted effects bypass most protection
Devastating board wipes at large Servant count
Wrath ability turns near-defeat into power spike
weaknesses: Expensive costs require many Red Shrines
Ancient Pantheon ⚡.counter: A Consecrated blocker in Defense Position can stall while you build. Force sacrifice effects remove their best attacker cheaply. Prophetic Order counters their most expensive Gods before they land.

Dharmic Path ☸.lore: The Dharmic Path encompasses Eastern spiritual traditions — Buddhist monks seeking enlightenment, Hindu Devas maintaining cosmic order, Taoist masters embodying nature's flow. Their Faith is green — of growing things, of rivers that carve stone through patience. They believe victory is achieved not by defeating enemies but by achieving such profound harmony that no enemy can gain a foothold.
figures: Compassion manifest; heals all allies every turn
Creates two Spirit tokens each turn; endless resources
Massive DEF; cannot be destroyed by combat
Reaching 10 Karma counters triggers alternate win
Nine-armed warrior; fights eight Servants simultaneously
Dharmic Path ☸.playstyle: Turns opponent's strengths against them. Maintains high DEF to survive aggression, accumulates life through Karma mechanics, and uses Karmic Return to convert damage into healing. Prefers long games.
strengths: Highest DEF values — hard to overcome in combat
Karmic Return makes damage strategies backfire
Sustainable resource generation
Strong in long games
weaknesses: Low ATK — cannot pressure opponent proactively
Pantheon's non-targeted effects bypass Karmic Return
Dharmic Path ☸.counter: Non-targeted mass damage. Ancient Pantheon's area effects cannot be Karmic-Returned. Direct Judgment ignores their life total. Effects preventing healing cut off their engine.

Covenant Keepers ⚖.lore: There are those who refuse the easy certainty of a single faith. The Covenant Keepers are diplomats, syncretics, scholars of comparative theology, and warriors who have sworn oaths to protect all sacred traditions equally. Their symbol is the scales — not because they are neutral, but because they understand that balance between all forces creates something greater than any one force could alone.
figures: Legendary; can use abilities of ANY faction's Servants
Gains abilities from each nearby faction's Servant
Knows every ability as an activated ability
Diplomatic Immunity aura; protects all your Servants
Builds Unity counters faster than any other card
Covenant Keepers ⚖.playstyle: The combo and synergy faction. Combines mechanics from multiple factions — sacrifice to fuel healing, card draw to find finishers. Bridge of Faith makes multi-faction costs trivial. Unity counters reward diversity.
strengths: Accesses mechanics from all factions — extreme versatility
Unity counter scaling makes late-game boards overwhelming
Bridge of Faith makes deck construction flexible
Best matchup spread — no hard faction counters
weaknesses: Consistency can be an issue with diverse card pool
Medium stats — not best at anything specifically
Covenant Keepers ⚖.counter: Target the Shrines. Covenant Keepers need multiple Faith types — disrupting their Shrine setup prevents multi-faction casting. Counter key Covenant cards before they hit the field.

Celestial Host: "By the Lord's command, none shall pass who bear the mark of sin."
"We do not fight with hatred — we fight with absolute love for what is right."
"The gates of heaven are not locked to keep darkness out. They are open to welcome the faithful in."
"I have seen the end of all things. This is not it."
"Even the fallen were once radiant. That is why we grieve as we strike them down."
"Heaven's army does not march to conquer. It marches to protect."
"In the light of the eternal, all shadows are temporary."
"We are the shield upon which corruption breaks."
Fallen Dominion: "Power without limit requires sacrifice without limit."
"We did not fall. We chose to fly in a different direction."
"Heaven rejected me. So I built something far more interesting."
"Every soul has a price. The question is: who names it?"
"I was made of light. Now I am made of purpose."
"Darkness does not destroy the light. It reveals what it was hiding."
"The fallen do not despair. They remember."
Prophetic Order: "I have seen where this path leads. That is why I stand here."
"The fool acts. The wise foresees, then acts."
"Every ending was written long before it arrived."
"We do not predict the future. We read what was always written."
"Knowledge is not power. Knowledge is the only power. Everything else is its shadow."
"The book was not written to be understood. It was written to be studied — forever."
"A word unspoken cannot be countered. A word spoken cannot be unspoken."
"I have no enemies. Only subjects of prophecy who have not yet arrived at their appointed place."
Ancient Pantheon: "I am not worshipped because I am kind. I am worshipped because I am inevitable."
"Mortals name the stars after us. We named ourselves after nothing — we simply are."
"Every civilization that forgets the old gods eventually learns why they should not have."
"I have watched worlds end. This one is no different."
"Legends are just memories that outlive the ones who made them."
"The gods do not die. We transform."
"Do not pray to me for mercy. Pray to me for victory. Those I can give."
Dharmic Path: "True strength is not destroying your enemy. True strength is not needing to."
"The river does not fight the stone. It flows around it — and the stone wears away."
"Peace is not the absence of conflict. It is the transcendence of it."
"Every breath is a prayer. Every moment, an offering."
"I have nothing to prove to those who measure with the wrong scales."
"The lotus blooms in muddy water. So do we."
"What you resist persists. What you accept transforms."
"Patience is not weakness. It is power held in reserve."
Covenant Keepers: "We do not choose between faiths. We carry them all."
"Unity is not agreement. Unity is the decision to act together despite disagreement."
"The bridge between our faiths is built from what we share, not from what divides us."
"I have prayed in every language. The divine has answered in all of them."
"A covenant made in good faith is stronger than any army."
"When the faiths stand together, what force in the universe can oppose them?"
"We do not ask which god you serve. We ask: are you willing to serve?"
"Every sacred text agrees: treat others as you would be treated."
